domain: glassmaking (Ravenscroft lead-crystal 1675: refractive-index + dispersion tuning via PbO substitution)
specific_mechanism: Ravenscroft replaced potash flux with PbO to overcome crizzling; lead content raises refractive index from n≈1.5 (ordinary glass) to n≈1.7-1.8 (up to 35% PbO). Dispersion (wavelength-dependent index) scales with the increased base index. The substitution simultaneously boosts (a) density, (b) refractive index, (c) dispersion — a triple-coupled materials property.
llm_application: Treat hidden-state activation channel scaling as a refractive-index property of the embedding 'medium'. Inject a learned 'lead-oxide-like' channel-weighting at chosen layers to increase representational 'density' (more information per dimension); this raises the channel-wise activation 'refractive index' and consequently the 'dispersion' (the variance in how different token-frequency components propagate). Feedback-attenuation: high-PbO layers cancel mid-frequency activation drift the same way Ravenscroft's lead substitution attenuated the crizzling feedback loop in glass.
novelty_claim: BAE (2509.20997, ICLR 2026 under review) addresses dense features via batch-autoencoder entropy training + consistent activation scaling. None proposes a 'refractive-index/dispersion'-style scaling that explicitly couples density-boost to representational dispersion as a feedback-attenuation mechanism. Open question whether this is substantively new or a re-description of feature-scale recalibration.
